nts1.d: Ziggy Devriendt is the DJ behind Nosedrip. Tune in once a month for an expert selection of any or all of the following: new age, new wave, French psych, minimal synth, post-punk and a whole lot else…

nts1.n: OKONKOLE Y TROMPA W/ PAM & MÉLANIE MATRANGA (R)
nts1.d:
My Favorite Things Will Never Rot. The first hour of this program loosely reflects on W.G. Sebald's 1995 novel 'The Rings of Saturn', as well as the hit tune from the Sound Of Music, 'My Favorite Things'. It is followed by a guest mix by French artist Mélanie Matranga.
It features extracts from Grant Gee's 2012 film 'Patience (After Sebald)'

nts2.d: Adam Cooper aka Foreigner heads the Foreign Hour show - a monthly offering of contemporary music from dembow to hip-hop shining light on the diaspora.
Monthly hub for club geographies, place-specific constructions, architectural musics and the production of sonic space and scale.

subtle.n: VIOLENTLYILL'S WAX HEAVY w/ GILLY MAN GIRO

subtle.d: Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Following his widely acclaimed breakout moment winning the 2013 DMC World Online Championships, Jon1st quickly built an international reputation as a ‘DJ’s DJ’. Not only proving himself as one of the world’s most diverse and musical turntablists, Jon also established himself as an on the pulse club DJ mixing across the alternative bass music spectrum and as an in demand live performer around the world, as well as recording acclaimed guest mixes for everyone from DJ Mag to Boiler Room to Ninja Tune’s Solid Steel to Mary Anne Hobbs to Keep Hush.

Established in 2013, eatmybeat is a hybrid record label & club brand that was brought together by a group of friends with a passion for electronic music and building community. With diverse tastes, they support and champion the underground bass music scene through events and releases on the label. Refusing to be pigeonholed into a specific sound, they showcase everything from hip-hop, bass & breaks, through to techno, jungle, dubstep and grime. As long as it's tasty and full fat, you best believe eatmybeat are snacking on it.
Born in Sunderland, based in London, Kitschko's productions and selections display a deep appreciation of UK club culture, and an affinity for late night, introspective sounds.

As a drummer since childhood, Chacho has always had an insatiable appetite for new and unique beats.
Combining his love of global percussion, dark grooves and deeper low end frequencies, 
his sets feature a blend of dubstep, grime, garage, breaks, hip hop and other flavours from around the world.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
